NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in the LORD Are like Mount Zion, Which cannot be moved, but abides forever.
NKJV.2: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, So the LORD surrounds His people From this time forth and forever.
NKJV.3: For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest On the land allotted to the righteous, Lest the righteous reach out their hands to iniquity.
NKJV.4: Do good, O LORD, to those who are good, And to those who are upright in their hearts.
NKJV.5: As for such as turn aside to their crooked ways, The LORD shall lead them away With the workers of iniquity. Peace be upon Israel!

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> of degrees<S>4609</S>. They that trust<S>982</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S> shall be as mount<S>2022</S> Zion<S>6726</S>, which cannot be removed<S>4131</S>, but abideth<S>3427</S> for ever<S>5769</S>.
KJV.2: As the mountains<S>2022</S> are round about<S>5439</S> Jerusalem<S>3389</S>, so the LORD<S>3068</S> is round about<S>5439</S> his people<S>5971</S> from henceforth even for<S>5704</S> ever<S>5769</S>.
KJV.3: For the rod<S>7626</S> of the wicked<S>7562</S> shall not rest<S>5117</S> upon the lot<S>1486</S> of the righteous<S>6662</S>; lest<S>4616</S> the righteous<S>6662</S> put forth<S>7971</S> their hands<S>3027</S> unto iniquity<S>5766</S>. <sup>the wicked: Heb. wickedness</sup>
KJV.4: Do good<S>2895</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, unto those that be good<S>2896</S>, and to them that are upright<S>3477</S> in their hearts<S>3826</S>.
KJV.5: As for such as turn aside<S>5186</S> unto their crooked ways<S>6128</S>, the LORD<S>3068</S> shall lead them forth<S>3212</S> with the workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S>: but peace<S>7965</S> shall be upon Israel<S>3478</S>.

ESV.1: Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved, but abides forever.
ESV.2: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so the LORD surrounds his people, from this time forth and forevermore.
ESV.3: For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest on the land allotted to the righteous, lest the righteous stretch out their hands to do wrong.
ESV.4: Do good, O LORD, to those who are good, and to those who are upright in their hearts!
ESV.5: But those who turn aside to their crooked ways the LORD will lead away with evildoers! Peace be upon Israel!

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in the LORD Are as Mount Zion, which cannot be moved but abides forever.
NASB.2: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, So the LORD surrounds His people From this time forth and forever.
NASB.3: For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest upon the land of the righteous, So that the righteous will not put forth their hands to do wrong.
NASB.4: Do good, O LORD, to those who are good And to those who are upright in their hearts.
NASB.5: But as for those who turn aside to their crooked ways, The LORD will lead them away with the doers of iniquity. Peace be upon Israel.

NLT.1:
Those who trust in the LORD are as secure as Mount Zion;
they will not be defeated but will endure forever.
NLT.2:
Just as the mountains surround Jerusalem,
so the LORD surrounds his people, both now and forever.
NLT.3:
The wicked will not rule the land of the godly,
for then the godly might be tempted to do wrong.
NLT.4:
O LORD, do good to those who are good,
whose hearts are in tune with you.
NLT.5:
But banish those who turn to crooked ways, O LORD.
Take them away with those who do evil.
May Israel have peace!

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in Yahweh are as Mount Zion, which can’t be moved, but remains forever.
WEB.2: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so Yahweh surrounds his people from this time forward and forever more.
WEB.3: For the scepter of wickedness won’t remain over the allotment of the righteous; so that the righteous won’t use their hands to do evil.
WEB.4: Do good, Yahweh, to those who are good, to those who are upright in their hearts.
WEB.5: But as for those who turn aside to their crooked ways, Yahweh will lead them away with the workers of iniquity. Peace be on Israel.

CSB17.1:
A song of ascents. [1] Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion.
It cannot be shaken; it remains forever. ⓔ
CSB17.2:
The mountains surround Jerusalem
and the LORD surrounds his people,
both now and forever. ⓕ
CSB17.3:
The scepter of the wicked will not remain
over the land allotted to the righteous,
so that the righteous will not apply their hands to injustice. ⓖ
CSB17.4:
Do what is good, LORD, to the good,
to those whose hearts are upright. ⓗ
CSB17.5:
But as for those who turn aside to crooked ways,
the LORD will banish them with the evildoers. ⓘ
Peace be with Israel. ⓙ

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in and rely on the LORD [with confident expectation]
Are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved but remains forever.
AMP.2:
As the mountains surround Jerusalem,
So the LORD surrounds His people
From this time forth and forever.
AMP.3:
For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest on the land of the righteous,
So that the righteous will not reach out their hands to do wrong.
AMP.4:
Do good, O LORD, to those who are good
And to those who are upright in their hearts.
AMP.5:
But as for those who turn aside to their crooked ways [in unresponsiveness to God],
The LORD will lead them away with those who do evil.
Peace be upon Israel.

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
Those who trust in God
are like Zion Mountain:
Nothing can move it, a rock-solid mountain
you can always depend on.
MSG.2:
Mountains encircle Jerusalem,
and God encircles his people—
always has and always will.
MSG.3:
The fist of the wicked
will never violate
What is due the righteous,
provoking wrongful violence.
MSG.4:
Be good to your good people, God,
to those whose hearts are right!
MSG.5:
God will round up the backsliders,
corral them with the incorrigibles.
Peace over Israel!

NIV.1:
Psalm 125
A song of ascents.
Those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion,
which cannot be shaken but endures forever.
NIV.2:
As the mountains surround Jerusalem,
so the Lord surrounds his people
both now and forevermore.
NIV.3:
The scepter of the wicked will not remain
over the land allotted to the righteous,
for then the righteous might use
their hands to do evil.
NIV.4:
Do good, O Lord, to those who are good,
to those who are upright in heart.
NIV.5:
But those who turn to crooked ways
the Lord will banish with the evildoers.
Peace be upon Israel.

NRSVCE.1:
Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion,
which cannot be moved, but abides forever.
NRSVCE.2:
As the mountains surround Jerusalem,
so the LORD surrounds his people,
from this time on and forevermore.
NRSVCE.3:
For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest
on the land allotted to the righteous,
so that the righteous might not stretch out
their hands to do wrong.
NRSVCE.4:
Do good, O LORD, to those who are good,
and to those who are upright in their hearts.
NRSVCE.5:
But those who turn aside to their own crooked ways
the LORD will lead away with evildoers.
Peace be upon Israel!

NET.1: Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion; it cannot be upended and will endure forever.
NET.2: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so the LORD surrounds his people, now and forevermore.
NET.3: Indeed, the scepter of a wicked king will not settle upon the allotted land of the godly. Otherwise the godly might do what is wrong.
NET.4: Do good, O LORD, to those who are good, to the morally upright!
NET.5: As for those who are bent on traveling a sinful path, may the LORD remove them, along with those who behave wickedly! May Israel experience peace!
